summary: Iteration 177 — Late-Encode closed-day sales + Close-Reminder SMS scheduler. Built a comprehensive backend test suite (16 tests) covering POST /api/unified-sale closed-day guard, all late-encode validations, GET /api/sales/late-encoded-since-close, routes.close_reminder importables (STAGES, tick_once, _build_branch_snapshot, _in_quiet_hours, send_zreport_finalized), and routes.sms.DEFAULT_TEMPLATES new keys. All 16 new tests PASS. Re-ran 70 existing regression tests (audit_pulse + audit_deep_analysis_phase1 + audit_center_93 + audit_center_clickable_96 + overage_reserve) → all 70 PASS. No backend code modifications required — all code paths behaved as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/close_reminder.py is well-structured (~452 lines), clean async, properly uses TenantCollection-aware helpers, dedup via close_reminder_log, quiet-hours guardrail, and zero-sales suppression. Good idempotency design.

routes/sales.py late-encode guard correctly orders checks (payment-type → days_back → cross-month → reason → PIN → daily-cap) so the user gets the most actionable error first. days_back=0 (today) is implicitly allowed — fine since today is unlikely to be 'closed' but worth a sanity test.

Cross-month rule rejects late-encoding into a previous calendar month (protects VAT). Combined with the 7-day cap, this means an encode within 1–7 days of month-end could fail twice; the test 'days_back_over_7' tolerates either error message — good defensive testing.
send_zreport_finalized is fired via _asyncio.create_task in daily_operations.py (non-blocking). Safe — failures only logged.

STAGES list has nine entries (two repeats of close_overdue_multi_day for day_offset 1 and 2). Spec asked for 7 stage keys; mapping to STAGES works because tests check stage_key membership not exact length. Behaviour matches PRD.
seed_data_creation: Per-test fixtures create transient daily_closings docs (with organization_id) and 5 seed late-encoded invoices for the daily-cap test. All cleaned up after each test via teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: When inserting test data for tenant-isolated collections (daily_closings, invoices, etc — see TENANT_COLLECTIONS in /app/backend/config.py), ALWAYS include organization_id in the inserted doc. The backend's TenantCollection wrapper fails closed and won't see docs lacking organization_id. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_late_encode_close_reminder_177.py as a template for late-encode/close-reminder regression in future iterations.
rca of the issue: No bugs. During test development, observed that direct PyMongo inserts of daily_closings without organization_id are invisible to the backend route (TenantCollection fail-closed) — adjusted fixtures to include organization_id. This is expected/correct behaviour from the iter-180 cross-tenant privacy fix, not a regression.
